plugin_desc: Eltex MES-series switch-specific SNMP collection (RADLAN/Marvell-based MIBs + ELTEX private OIDs, IANA PEN 89/35265). In addition to standard IF-MIB interface metrics, it collects Eltex private OIDs for device health: CPU utilization (rlCpuUtilDuringLast5Minutes), system memory (total/free), chassis temperature (rlPhdUnitEnvParamTempSensorValue, dedicated typed column), fan state (rlEnvMonFanState) and power-supply state (rlPhdUnitEnvParamMainPSStatus). Fan/PSU raw codes are normalized via telegraf processors.enum to 1=healthy / 2=fault. Memory utilization is computed from (total-free)/total. This is the first switch brand exposing real temperature/fan/PSU because Eltex provides them as typed value columns (no per-row type filtering needed).
name: Switch

metrics.name: device_cpu_usage
metrics.description: This metric reports the CPU utilization of the Eltex MES device control plane (RADLAN-rndMng rlCpuUtilDuringLast5Minutes, five-minute average), normalized to 0-100. Sustained high CPU usage can indicate control-plane overload, routing instability, or attack traffic, and may lead to slow management response or packet processing delays.

metrics.name: device_memory_total
metrics.description: This metric reports the total size in bytes of the Eltex MES system memory (ELTEX private OID 35265.41.1.2.1.1.3). Together with the free bytes it determines overall memory utilization.

metrics.name: device_memory_free
metrics.description: This metric reports the number of free bytes in the Eltex MES system memory (ELTEX private OID 35265.41.1.2.1.1.7). Low free memory may indicate memory pressure from oversized routing/MAC/ARP tables or leaks.

metrics.name: device_memory_usage
metrics.description: This metric reports overall memory utilization of the Eltex MES device, computed as (total - free) / total * 100. High memory usage may indicate memory leaks, oversized tables, or insufficient capacity, and can cause instability or failed allocations.

metrics.name: device_temperature_celsius
metrics.description: This metric reports the chassis temperature in degrees Celsius of the Eltex MES device (RADLAN-Physicaldescription rlPhdUnitEnvParamTempSensorValue, a dedicated typed temperature column), aggregated as the maximum across chassis/stack units. High temperatures can indicate cooling failure or airflow obstruction and risk thermal shutdown.

metrics.name: device_fan_state
metrics.description: This metric reports the fan state of the Eltex MES device (RADLAN-HWENVIROMENT rlEnvMonFanState), normalized via telegraf processors.enum so that 1 = healthy (OK / not-present) and 2 = fault. Aggregated as the maximum across fans so any faulty fan surfaces. A value greater than 1 indicates a fan failure that can lead to overheating.

metrics.name: device_psu_state
metrics.description: This metric reports the power-supply state of the Eltex MES device (RADLAN-Physicaldescription rlPhdUnitEnvParamMainPSStatus), normalized via telegraf processors.enum so that 1 = healthy (Normal / not-present / backing-up) and 2 = fault (Warning / Critical / Shutdown / not-functioning). Aggregated as the maximum across units so any failed PSU surfaces. A value greater than 1 indicates a power-supply problem.

metrics.name: interface_ifHCInOctets
metrics.description: This metric indicates the average rate of bytes received over the past 5 minutes, measured in bytes per second, from the 64-bit IF-MIB ifHCInOctets counter (ifXTable). The 64-bit counter avoids the rapid wrap of the 32-bit ifInOctets on high-speed switch ports, keeping the rate accurate.

metrics.name: interface_ifHCOutOctets
metrics.description: This metric calculates the average rate of bytes sent over the past 5 minutes, measured in bytes per second, from the 64-bit IF-MIB ifHCOutOctets counter (ifXTable). The 64-bit counter avoids the rapid wrap of the 32-bit ifOutOctets on high-speed switch ports, keeping the rate accurate.
